resources.string: The moss cradles every ache, whispering that you have done enough.

The meadow is warm with a holy light. Moss receives the body as if it had studied every burden. Vines take the weapon gently, not as chains, but as permission to let it fall. A lullaby washes over every scar and declares each struggle finished. There are no commands here. Only kindness. Rest for a moment. Let your eyes close. Let them forget how to open.
